rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
We will analyse the three parts of (Mario Puzo's and) Francis Ford Coppola's "Godfather"-Saga focussing on the narrative, i. e. non-technical aspects of the movies. Topics covered in discussions include:
- Crime, violence, and the movies
- Immigration and organised crime
- Organised crime and family
- Crime families and the role of women
- Organised crime and politics
- American organised crime and racism
- Crime and capitalism
etc.
aims:
The main course aim is to enable students to analyse movies and to understand their cultural background and impact.
Another aim is to give students an opportunity to USE (rather than to LEARN) English for a specific purpose.
goals: To be able to analyse film narratives as narrations and retell them in English
To be able to place film narratives within their cultural background and explain one's views in English
To be able to interpret the "topics" of a movie and express one's thoughts in English
To be able to evaluate a given movie (on a very rudimentary level) and express one's opinions in English

plan.text:
Orientation
Introduction: Why do we watch violent crime movies?

Godfather Part I
Analysis Scenes 20 - 22
Student Presentations

Godfather Part I
Student Presentations
Movie Evaluation

Godfather Part II
Analysis Scenes Disc 1, 1 - 8

Godfather Part II
Analysis Scenes Disc 1, 9 - 16

Godfather Part II
Analysis Scenes Disc 2, 1 - 8

Godfather Part II Disc 2, 9 -13
Student Presentations
Movie Evaluation

Godfather Part III
Student Presentations
Movie Evaluation
Final Discussion: Asked once more: Why do we watch violent crime movies?

textbooks:
Necessary materials will be provided.
Participants could use the following material to prepare:
The Godfather, Part I (1972, DVD etc.)
The Godfather, Part II (1974, DVD etc.)
The Godfather, Part III (1990, DVD etc.)
Mario Puzo: The Godfather (1969, novel)
Jenny M. Jones: The Annotated Godfather. The Complete Screenplay (Black Dog & Leventhal Publishers, 2021)
prereq: Participants must be able to understand and debate freely in English.
prep: Participants should know at least the movie "The Godfather, Part I" at the outset and watch the sequels as the seminar progresses. Having read either the original novel "The Godfather" by Mario Puzo or the annotated screenplay (see below) would be desirable.

officeHour.name: Stefan Hug
